methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected pollen data, including calculating relative abundances, creating pollen diagrams, determining pollen zones, and conducting multivariate analysis to detect compositional changes.

steps.description: We used the pollen sum to calculate relative abundances of each taxon, which was expressed as percentages.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative abundances of pollen taxa

steps.description: We assigned pollen taxa to a vegetation type based on fieldwork and bibliographic references.
steps.searchable_summary: assign pollen taxa to vegetation types
outputs: pollen taxa assigned to vegetation types

steps.description: We created pollen diagrams using pollen percentages with Tilia and Tilia Graph.
steps.searchable_summary: create pollen diagrams using Tilia

steps.description: Pollen zones were determined using the stratigraphically-constrained incremental sum of square clustering (CONISS 1.5.12).
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen zones using CONISS

steps.description: We performed a multivariate analysis to detect differences in the composition of the pollen assemblage between different pollen intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multivariate analysis on pollen assemblage

steps.description: A detrended correspondence analysis (DCA) was conducted using all pollen taxa present at abundances higher than 2% and recorded in more than five samples.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct DCA on pollen taxa

steps.description: We performed a DCA to detect major compositional changes and species turnover of dominant taxa through time.
steps.searchable_summary: detect compositional changes using DCA

steps.description: The Vegan package of R statistical software was used to perform the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: use Vegan package in R for analysis

methods.description: Procedures for retrieving and processing sediment cores, including lithology characterization, organic and inorganic content determination, pollen and charcoal analysis, and core chronology construction.

steps.description: In 2016, one sediment core was retrieved from an Andean peatland in 50-cm sections with a Russian corer, to a maximum depth of 550 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve sediment core using Russian corer

steps.description: Core sections were packed and transported to the palynology lab at CEAZA in La Serena, Chile.
steps.searchable_summary: transport core sections to lab

steps.description: We characterized the lithology of the sediment cores based on field assessments of the stratigraphy, and later observations derived from X-ray radiographs.
steps.searchable_summary: characterize lithology using stratigraphy and X-ray

steps.description: We determined the content of organic and inorganic matter by collecting 1-cm³ sediment subsamples every centimeter and conducting Loss-on-Ignition (LOI).
steps.searchable_summary: determine organic/inorganic content using LOI

steps.description: We collected sediment sub-samples every 4-7 cm for pollen and microscopic charcoal analysis (69 samples in total).
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment sub-samples for analysis

steps.description: We added 2 Lycopodium tablets to calculate pollen concentration and pollen influx.
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium tablets for pollen concentration

steps.description: We used standardized pollen techniques to process soil samples, including HCl, KOH, HF, and acetolysis.
steps.searchable_summary: process soil samples using standardized pollen techniques

steps.description: Optical microscopy (400×) was used to identify pollen grains.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen grains using optical microscopy

steps.description: In each sample, we identified a minimum of 300 pollen grains, excluding Cyperaceae and other local aquatic taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: identify minimum 300 pollen grains per sample

steps.description: We identified pollen grains using palynological atlases, pollen keys, and the pollen reference collections at CEAZA and the Herbario Nacional de Bolivia.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen grains using reference materials

steps.description: Microcharcoal concentration was determined in the same samples as counted for pollen and spore analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: determine microcharcoal concentration

steps.description: We counted microscopic charcoal particles larger than 10 µm size and Lycopodium spores on 200 fields of view selected randomly.
steps.searchable_summary: count charcoal particles and Lycopodium spores

steps.description: Microcharcoal particles were categorized either as woody or herbaceous particles based on their morphology.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize microcharcoal particles by morphology

steps.description: We identified herbaceous charcoal as particles with presence of stomata within epidermal cells and a flat surface, and we considered that they come from grasses or other monocots.
steps.searchable_summary: identify herbaceous charcoal based on morphology

steps.description: We estimated charcoal concentration values by counting the number of Lycopodium spores.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate charcoal concentration using Lycopodium spores

steps.description: We constructed a core chronology by using 14 C accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS) dates on seven bulk organic samples.
steps.searchable_summary: construct core chronology using AMS dates

steps.description: All samples for radiocarbon dating were analyzed at the DirectAMS radiocarbon lab.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples for radiocarbon dating

steps.description: We calibrated the radiocarbon dates by using the Southern Hemisphere calibration curve SHCAL13.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon dates using SHCAL13

steps.description: We calibrated the samples and generated a Bayesian age-depth model with the Bacon package version 2.2 in R.
steps.searchable_summary: generate Bayesian age-depth model using Bacon package

steps.description: The model was built under the assumption that the top of the sequence corresponds to the year of core retrieval (2016 CE).
steps.searchable_summary: assume top of sequence is 2016 CE for model
